races.race_name: Racing Welfare Reminiscence Day Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Mount Ruapehu
runners.spotlight: Three 6f AW wins this year for new yard and short-headed at Hamilton (5f, good) eight days ago when back on turf; there can be optimism that he will be fine back up to 7f and he's a strong contender.
runners.comment: In fine form over 5f/6f for new yard; can be optimism that he will be fine back up to 7f

runners.name: Asian Journey
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden who didn't look an easy ride when 2l fourth of nine over C&D (good) last month and needs to prove he can get the job done; however, he often runs well and might not be far away.
runners.comment: 15-race maiden who needs to prove he can get the job done, but he often runs well

runners.name: Coconut Bay
runners.spotlight: Two of last summer's three wins came over C&D (good/good to firm) and she returned to form when third at Ayr (7f, good to firm) last Tuesday on her second start of the season; she's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner who returned to form at Ayr last week; on the shortlist

runners.name: Travis
runners.spotlight: Just two wins from 31 starts but this front-runner often runs well and he was a close second on his reappearance at Newcastle (7f, AW) in March; had to work hard early to lead from wide draw at Thirsk (1m, good) last time so that fifth of 13 was a creditable performance; he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Front-runner who has a modest strike-rate but arrives in good heart and is one to consider

runners.name: Without Delay
runners.spotlight: Two-time C&D winner, the latest last July when 5lb higher; however, she has to better what she's achieved on her two runs this year.
runners.comment: Two-time C&D winner; well handicapped but she's yet to shine this season

runners.name: Crocodile Power
runners.spotlight: His last four wins have come over 6f here (good and soft), the latest last October; reappeared with 5l third of 12 over C&D last month but only seventh of nine over C&D (also good) since; 6f seems his optimum trip.
runners.comment: His last four wins have come over 6f here but that seems to be his optimum trip

runners.name: Filey Beach
runners.spotlight: Two wins last August/September, the first of them over C&D (good), and he was back on song when fourth of 11 at Musselburgh (7f, good) 12 days ago on his second outing of the year; every chance he'll be thereabouts.
runners.comment: C&D winner who ran well at Musselburgh recently; every chance he'll be thereabouts

runners.name: Langholm
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who may have needed last month's reappearance run at Thirsk, but this 10yo was soundly beaten and his sole win last season came in a classified race (C&D); others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner but this 10yo was soundly beaten on last month's comeback run

runners.name: Hostility
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden who could do with settling better; however, 1m stretched him at Ripon last time and prior to that he was a fairly encouraging fourth over this C&D (good) on his reappearance; not ruled out back down in trip.
runners.comment: 0-15 and could do with settling better, but he's not ruled out back down in trip

runners.name: How Impressive
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Kempton (7f, AW) two starts ago and he has a 9lb lower mark to work with on turf; however, he's been inconsistent this year and his sole turf win (from 15 attempts) came back in 2023.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on this year's AW form but he's inferior on turf, on which he's 1-15

runners.name: White Umbrella
runners.spotlight: Won here in 2023 and she scored twice at Wolverhampton (7f/6f, AW) last June; good third at Wolverhampton in March but not at the same level there on either start since and she needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: Good third at Wolverhampton in March but not at the same level there since

runners.name: Kings Merchant
runners.spotlight: He needs to produce a bigger performance than he's managed over 6f on his four starts this spring but he hasn't been running badly and is dropping down the weights; showed promise over 7f at Carlisle last September and the step back up in trip may be a positive move; possible player.
runners.comment: Dropping down the weights and the return to 7f may be a positive; possible player

runners.name: Carlton And Co
runners.spotlight: She won three times in the second half of last year (6f/7f, good/AW) and her current mark isn't necessarily beyond her; a market check is advised but she may need this comeback run.
runners.comment: Three wins in the second half of 2025 but it's possible she'll need this comeback run

runners.name: Patontheback
runners.spotlight: He lacks a recent run and was below par more often than not last year; however, he reappears on a handy mark and, with his stable going well, it will be no surprise if this C&D winner is in the mix.
runners.comment: Inconsistent last year but returns on a handy mark and with his stable going well

races.race_name: Racing Welfare Supporting Mental Health Awareness Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sandy Craic
runners.spotlight: 0-5 and has had more chances than any of these but she's been steadily progressive and ran well when third in a maiden at Southwell (6f, AW) last month; good second over 7f on turf at York last July; leading form claims.
runners.comment: 0-5 but third at Southwell last month and holds leading form claims

runners.name: Different Opinions
runners.spotlight: With a top trainer and he was a promising second on debut at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in March; however, he's gone backwards since and was disappointing at Ripon (6f, good to soft) last month on his first turf outing.
runners.comment: Promising debut at Chelmsford in March but he's gone backwards since

runners.name: Big Shot Veto
runners.spotlight: Belied 33-1 odds when 2l third on debut at Leicester (7f, good to soft) last September; underwhelming at Southwell (7f, AW) the following month but he's a major player if rediscovering his initial promise.
runners.comment: Promising third on the first of two starts last autumn; major player on that initial form

runners.name: Vega Storm
runners.spotlight: Kept on for promising third on debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) in March; failed to build on that when third at Ripon (6f, good to soft) but the step up in trip could be a good move and he retains potential.
runners.comment: Promising third on debut; failed to build on it next time but he retains potential

runners.name: Lightening Lad
runners.spotlight: Recovered from slow start to finish second on debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) last November; disappointing there (1m) in December but he remains one to be interested in given the promise shown on his first start.
runners.comment: Runner-up on debut last November (7f, AW); disappointing next time but remains of interest

runners.name: Highland Shah
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability at Newcastle (7f, AW) in December on his first two starts, finishing third when last seen; gelded since; his pedigree provides hope that we have yet to see the best of him; a hood goes on; chance.
runners.comment: He has a useful pedigree and he displayed ability on his first two starts; gelded; chance

runners.name: Pennine Way
runners.spotlight: Beaten around 6l when fifth then fourth at Southwell (6f, AW) last autumn on her first two starts; improvement is necessary but she's from a family connections have done very well with, and her in-form southern trainer is 5-9 at Catterick; wears a first-time hood; interesting.
runners.comment: Improvement needed on third start but southern trainer is 5-9 at Catterick; interesting

runners.name: Zarvali
runners.spotlight: Ran four times for Johnny Murtagh last year, finishing second in a nursery at Fairyhouse in September when last seen; subsequently sold for 25,000euros and gelded; upset in stalls and withdrawn from intended stable debut at Thirsk last month (40-1 at the time); improvement is necessary.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Fairyhouse nursery last September; 40-1 when withdrawn at Thirsk last month

runners.name: Tickhill Flyer
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to three winners but he was well beaten at big odds last month on his first two starts.
runners.comment: Half-brother to three winners but well beaten at big odds last month on first two starts

runners.name: Pacific Glory
runners.spotlight: 33-1 when fourth of five, beaten just under 7l, on recent debut at Redcar (7f, good to firm); that performance gives this half-brother to five winners something to build on but his pedigree suggests he'll come into his own when tackling a longer trip.
runners.comment: 4th of 5 on debut; pedigree suggests he'll come into his own when tackling a longer trip

runners.name: Alcanzor
runners.spotlight: He ran twice last autumn and finished down the field at big odds each time (1m/7f, good/AW); he'll be of greater interest when sent handicapping.
runners.comment: He ran twice last autumn and finished down the field at big odds each time

races.race_name: Mick Rose Turns 70 Handicap

runners.name: Laravie
runners.spotlight: Unplaced when just 2-1 for last month's Flat handicap debut at Lingfield (2m, AW) but justified favouritism in good style at Salisbury (1m6f, good to firm) subsequently; did it in good style and she still appeals as being well treated under a 4lb penalty, especially in view of her hurdling ability.
runners.comment: Won in good style at Salisbury and still looks well treated under a 4lb penalty

runners.name: Stellarmasterpiece
runners.spotlight: Three course wins over 1m4f last year and she kept on for a good third over 1m6f here last time, providing optimism that this trip will be within range; there's every chance she'll be in the mix.
runners.comment: Loves it here and kept on for 1m6f third here last time; every chance she'll be in the mix

runners.name: Bouboule
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) in January but he's struggled over hurdles and on the Flat subsequently and is now 0-17; hopes are pinned on wind surgery prompting a major turnaround.
runners.comment: 17-race maiden; hopes are pinned on wind surgery prompting a major turnaround

runners.name: Himself
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row over 1m6f on AW early this year and he may have been ready for a break when below par in February when last seen; 0-12 on turf and he needs to transfer his improvement back to grass, but he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Three AW wins early this year; has to transfer improvement back to grass but not ruled out

runners.name: Arctic Fox
runners.spotlight: 10yo who snapped a losing sequence at Carlisle last June and reappeared with a good second here (1m6f, good) last month; she should be fine back up in trip and can be bang there if backing up that latest performance.
runners.comment: 10yo who reappeared with good second here last month; bang there if backing that up

runners.name: Stand Strong
runners.spotlight: He lacks a recent outing and was inconsistent last year but reappears on a competitive mark and his trainer won this last year; ran well last May on his sole previous visit to Catterick and could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Lacks a recent outing but he's on a competitive mark and his trainer won this last year
runners.owner: A Whillans, C Spark, J Wright

runners.name: Zimmerman
runners.spotlight: The odd sign of spark last season and he may have needed his recent comeback run; down to a dangerous mark but this 7yo has been regressive and is winless since July 2024; cheekpieces return for first time since last May.
runners.comment: Down to a dangerous mark but this 7yo has been regressive and is winless since July 2024

runners.name: Wasthatok
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles at Newcastle last November and when back on the Flat at Wolverhampton (2m, AW) in March; badly hampered and unseated over hurdles next time; still has mileage in his mark judged on old Flat form; respected.
runners.comment: Won on last run in this code and there remains mileage in his mark judged on old Flat form

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: Aurora's Doublesix
runners.spotlight: Achieved very little across her first 11 starts but in first-time blinkers (retained) she ran well for a long way in a recent novice at Doncaster (5f, good to firm); interesting contender back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Blinkers prompted positive response last time; interesting contender back in a handicap

runners.name: Irish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year, starting with this race (good to firm), and he's been running well in the main on Southwell AW this spring; solid claims.
runners.comment: Won this 12 months ago and he's been running well in the main on AW of late; solid claims

runners.name: Ninety Nine
runners.spotlight: Last season ended with two poor runs and she's without a win since October 2024; however, that came over C&D and her best performance last season also came here; not written off on her reappearance.
runners.comment: Without a win since October 2024 but her course form offers hope; makes reappearance

runners.name: Lady Bouquet
runners.spotlight: Below par on final few outings in the winter but she may have been ready for a break; posted a C&D win and a good 6f second here last summer and is well treated on that form; capable of having a say.
runners.comment: Well treated on last summer's course form; returns from a break & capable of having a say

runners.name: Golden Prosperity
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent this year and not at the top of his game on his last two starts; however, before those he won over this C&D and perhaps the return to this track will be a positive; in the mix if so.
runners.comment: Not at the top of his game on his last two starts but before those he won over this C&D

runners.name: Siviez
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has dropped down the weights but she's finished unplaced this spring in her first four handicaps and needs something extra.
runners.comment: Unplaced this spring in her first four handicaps and she needs something extra

runners.name: Newyorkstateofmind
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner who went close at Newcastle (5f, AW) last September and races off 7lb lower today; however, last season ended tamely and this 9yo finished down the field on last month's reappearance.
runners.comment: Three-time turf winner who has dropped down the weights but needs to get back on track

runners.name: Hi Hoh Tonto
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden who has been unable to get competitive on his two runs this spring for a new trainer.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden who has been unable to get competitive on his two runs this spring

runners.name: Monticristo Boy
runners.spotlight: 5f AW winner for Nigel Tinkler; form went the wrong way last year and he's changed yards since his last run in August; on a reduced mark and market check advised on stable debut but he returns with something to prove.
runners.comment: Market check advised on stable debut but he was struggling for form when last seen

runners.name: Soul Seeker
runners.spotlight: Down to a basement mark and he's one to watch in the betting, but it's been a long time since this regressive 9yo has been competitive.
runners.comment: Down to a basement mark but a long time since this regressive 9yo has been competitive

runners.name: Hyrcanian
runners.spotlight: One of her better efforts when fifth of 11 over C&D in October when last seen but she's 0-14 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: One of her better efforts when fifth over C&D last October but 0-14 & others are preferred

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Free Pic
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) last month when back on turf and back down in trip and she followed up over 1m6f here; clearly thriving and has strong claims.
runners.comment: Two wins here last month and this thriving mare has strong claims

runners.name: Falaise Blanc
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 19 attempts came off this mark at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last August and he's also effective on turf; however, he's been soundly beaten on his three outings this spring.
runners.comment: Won off this mark on AW last August but soundly beaten on his three outings this spring

runners.name: Inspiring Speeches
runners.spotlight: Won at Beverley (1m2f, soft) last July and followed up over this C&D (good); tailed off here next time and down the field on last month's comeback at Beverley, but he may have needed that latest run; not ruled out.
runners.comment: C&D winner who may have needed his comeback run at Beverley and is not ruled out

runners.name: Raysham
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-8 when always behind on last month's reappearance at Nottingham (1m, good); however, he could be sharper for that run and he twice kept on for third over 1m2f last autumn (good to soft), shaping each time as though 1m4f would be well worth another go; possible player.
runners.comment: The return to 1m4f could be a positive on his second start of the season

runners.name: Lawmans Blis
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner who landed a novice hurdle last March; it's been slim pickings in the main since and he was soundly beaten on his recent stable debut at Southwell; however, he was sent off the 4-1 joint-favourite and it will be interesting to see if his backers return.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on recent stable debut on AW but interesting to see if his backers return

runners.name: Sassy Glory
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (1m, AW) last September and she's been effective over this far and on turf in the past; however, she's won just one of her 28 races and has to improve upon this year's form (1m-1m2f).
runners.comment: AW winner last September but poor strike-rate and has to improve upon this year's form
runners.owner: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club

runners.name: Rock Armour
runners.spotlight: Just one win from his 25 starts but he was a good third over C&D last month and didn't see out 1m6f when fifth to Free Pic here last time; each-way claims back down in trip.
runners.comment: Close 3rd over C&D last month then didn't see out 1m6f here; each-way claims back at 1m4f

runners.name: The Pug
runners.spotlight: He's posted a couple of respectable fourth placings on AW in 2026 but is inconsistent and has won just once since 2023; others are more compelling.
runners.comment: A couple of respectable runs this year but he's inconsistent & others are more compelling

runners.name: Muhib
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 21 starts and he's had several opportunities off a similar mark to today's, but he's been running pretty well of late and was third of seven at Beverley last Monday; might have an each-way shout.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden after 21 races but running pretty well of late; might have each-way shout

runners.name: Prince Hector
runners.spotlight: Kept to AW in the main nowadays and winless since last March; however, he ran well last August when he last tackled turf and the return of cheekpieces (replacing blinkers) may well be in his favour; also has a first-time tongue-tie; could be involved.
runners.comment: The returning cheekpieces may well be a positive and he could be involved

runners.name: Desert Quest
runners.spotlight: Well treated on his 2024 exploits and, having returned from a long absence in February, it's possible he's working his way back into form; however, this 8yo is not easy to fancy on the evidence of this year's two runs.
runners.comment: 8yo who is not easy to fancy on the evidence of this year's two runs
runners.owner: A Whillans, C Spark, J Wright

runners.name: Lady Buttercup
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles and hasn't threatened on her first three Flat starts; however, this bumper winner goes up in trip off a lowly opening Flat mark and Hollie Doyle has been booked; no surprise to see a good run.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who goes up in trip off lowly opening Flat mark; Hollie Doyle booked

runners.name: Tracker Issue
runners.spotlight: On a career-low mark and he's one to watch in the betting following a break; went close when 11-2 at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) last November but it's disconcerting he was tailed off there on his next two outings.
runners.comment: One to watch in the betting following a break but tailed off on AW on last two outings

runners.name: Stitching Wheel
runners.spotlight: Displayed early promise in France but unable to make an impact on her two runs this spring and she needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: Displayed early promise in France but unable to make an impact on her two runs this spring

races.race_name: Racing Again 21st May Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: North West Gal
runners.spotlight: Dropped to 5f after making her debut over 6f but she found plenty when winning a Newcastle AW maiden in November; the return to this trip on her handicap debut, having earlier shown she acts on this track, seems likely to suit if ready to do herself justice.
runners.comment: Found plenty to win 5f maiden on Newcastle AW in November; likely to stay 6f; h'cap debut

runners.name: Homestrait
runners.spotlight: Busy 2yo whose two wins came over 7f, in a maiden and a seller; creditable show from the front on Southwell AW when reappearing but down the field there since and looks vulnerable off her current mark.
runners.comment: Busy 2yo whose two wins came over 7f, in a maiden and a seller; vulnerable in handicaps

runners.name: Pull The Rug
runners.spotlight: Improved form when returned to 6f at Leicester (good to soft) in September, but, up 6lb, was soon pulling hard on Newcastle AW next time and paid the price; might be aiming for more cover this time and too soon to write off now back on turf.
runners.comment: Improved to win at Leicester (6f) as 2yo; pulled hard and faded on AW in October

runners.name: Miss Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Four wins at 5f-7f in 2025, including here; back on her latest winning mark and she ran well over C&D two starts ago so can't be ruled out, albeit she often leads so will need things to fall right from this draw.
runners.comment: On latest winning mark; often leads so needs things to fall right from widest draw

runners.name: Betties Bay
runners.spotlight: Turf winner as 2yo; missed all of 2025 but she's made hay off a much-reduced mark on AW for new yard, not needing much urging to complete the 6f hat-trick at Southwell in January; up 4lb but her best handicap form for previous yard was gained off a higher mark than today's; much respected.
runners.comment: Missed 2025; made hay on AW (6f) for new yard off much-reduced mark; still well treated

runners.name: Surprised
runners.spotlight: Won Beverley novice (about 7f) as a 2yo and ran well over 7f on this track subsequently; well held in all three handicaps, though, including on yard debut back at Beverley 18 days ago; trip query dropped to 6f and might need another run or two.
runners.comment: Won Beverley novice (about 7f) as 2yo; well held in handicaps, including for new yard

runners.name: Arctic Summer
runners.spotlight: A 2yo winner over 6f (very soft) in France but she's been high in the weights since trained in Britain and little sign of that changing in two runs this year; down another 2lb but likely that it isn't enough.
runners.comment: 2yo winner in France; high in weights since trained in Britain and well held this year

runners.name: Lily Pearl
runners.spotlight: All four runs at 6f on tracks with a stiff finish and she looked well suited by the stamina test when successful on handicap debut at Pontefract (good to soft; has handled good to firm) in September; raised 3lb and off since but should do better as a 3yo.
runners.comment: All 4 runs at 6f, stayed on to win handicap debut at Pontefract in September; off since

races.race_name: Dog Friendly Raceday Tues 2nd June Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Delgany Deadline
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Leicester (2m7f, soft) in February before posting a good second of four in 3m1f handicap at Plumpton (good); the drop back to 2m4f was not ideal there last time; better expected here.
runners.comment: In good form over fences until the drop back to 2m4f found him out at Plumpton

runners.name: Cave Article
runners.spotlight: Winning for the third time over fences when successful over C&D last June (good); back on a good mark and ran okay on his return from a break last month.
runners.comment: Back on a good mark and ran okay on his return from a break last month

runners.name: Huckleberry Sting
runners.spotlight: Poor five-race maiden over hurdles but this winning pointer was a different proposition when winning by 5l on chase debut at Bangor (3m, good; second run in this headgear combination); could cope with the 10lb rise.
runners.comment: Won by 5l on his switch to fences at Bangor; could cope with the 10lb rise

runners.name: Queens Wish
runners.spotlight: Ex-Skelton mare who is 1-9 for current yard; not disgraced in a better race at Warwick (3m1f, good) last time and could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in a better race at Warwick last time and could have a part to play

runners.name: Tom Desjy
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 6yo; close third of five on chase/handicap debut at Fontwell (2m5f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) but was looking in a bit of bother when unseating four out at Uttoxeter (3m, good; favourite); still lightly raced and Sean Bowen rides again.
runners.comment: In a spot of bother when unseating at Uttoxeter but he's still lightly raced

runners.name: Crac De Megaudais
runners.spotlight: Won 2m4f hurdle at the start of 2025 and made a fairly encouraging chase debut when third of six at Newton Abbot (2m, good) one year ago; returned from break with three disappointing runs from November to January, though, and has questions to answer on first attempt at 3m; hood refitted.
runners.comment: Three disappointing runs in November-January and has questions to answer at 3m

runners.name: Warner's Cross
runners.spotlight: Has won over hurdles and finished second as a chaser; however, pretty poor in both disciplines since returning from a long absence.
runners.comment: Has been pretty poor in both disciplines since returning from a long absence

races.race_name: Book The Bistro At Southwell Racecourse Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rule Me In
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles on rules debut in February 2025, but disappointing since switched to chasing in the autumn (six attempts); he was beaten 40l+ over about 2m4f on both outings in March.
runners.comment: Disappointing since switched to chasing in the autumn (six attempts)

runners.name: Seaview Rock
runners.spotlight: Won his maiden on heavy ground before racing too freely and not getting home when made favourite for his handicap debut (2m1f, good to soft); has further to go now on this switch to fences but he remains unexposed.
runners.comment: Maiden winner; raced too freely on handicap debut; soon sent over fences

runners.name: Doc McCoy
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 5-13 over hurdles and he's rated 10lb lower as a chaser (0-5); however, hasn't offered a lot in this discipline and it's over a year since he last went chasing.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler but hasn't offered a lot over fences and a while since he tried them

runners.name: Dunkerque
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner in Denmark and now a dual winner over hurdles after narrowly taking a small-field novice at Newcastle last month (2m4f, good); could be on a fair mark for this chasing debut but he'll need to take to fences.
runners.comment: Could be on a fair mark for this chasing debut but he'll need to take to fences

runners.name: Kara Sacre
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who was placed in maiden hurdles for Paul Nolan; had pulled himself to the front when falling four out in a Ffos Las maiden in February; perhaps fences will settle him down and ability is much respected; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Can pull hard; perhaps fences will settle him down and ability is much respected

races.race_name: Summer Fun & Makers Market Raceday 27th July Mares' Open Maiden NH Flat Race (Cat 3) (GBB) (Div I)

runners.name: Crystal Glade
runners.spotlight: Well-related filly who was beaten 8l at Market Rasen (good; 9-1) when showing signs of inexperience; can do better here.
runners.comment: Well-related filly who was beaten 8l at Market Rasen when showing signs of inexperience

runners.name: For Her Glory
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to winners Washington (useful bumper/2m hurdle) and Gold Leader (2m7f hurdle); dam unraced sister to 2m hurdle winner Mobile Sizer; has to be of interest for powerful yard.
runners.comment: Half-sister to useful winning hurdler Washington; has to be of strong interest
runners.owner: The Cheltenham & South West Racing Club

runners.name: Marie d'Altalinara
runners.spotlight: 70,000euros 3yo; seventh foal; half-sister to French winners Marie Coastala (useful 2m1f chase/2m2f hurdle) and Don Look Coco (2m2f-2m5f hurdle/chase); dam French 2m1f/2m3f hurdle winner; no surprise to see her make a bold bid for top connections.
runners.comment: Cost 70,000euros and no surprise to see her make a bold bid for top connections

runners.name: Go Hr
runners.spotlight: £27,000 3yo; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Ilovethenightlife; market can guide.
runners.comment: £27,000 3yo; dam unraced half-sister to 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Ilovethenightlife

runners.name: Royale Blue
runners.spotlight: Not far behind good horses when fifth at Chepstow 14 months ago, though they did go very steady; has since moved yards; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Moved yards since finishing fifth in a steadily run Chepstow bumper 14 months ago

runners.name: Mighty Harmony

runners.name: Alazwar
runners.spotlight: 7lb lower than for his Newbury win last summer; second of 11 at Lingfield (1m, AW) last month but that has been followed by two heavy defeats; comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: Capable on his day but he needs to bounce back from two heavy defeats

runners.name: Thursday
runners.spotlight: Won twice on fast ground in the summer of 2024, including at this track (extended 1m3f); she hasn't stood much racing since, seen just four times since September 2024, and there are major question marks over how much of that old ability she retains.
runners.comment: Course winner (extended 1m3f) but just 4 runs since September 2024 and well held each time

runners.name: Hengest
runners.spotlight: Stayed on nicely into fourth in an Epsom novice (8.5f, good) 13 days ago, his second run for new connections; open to improvement now handicapping over 1m2f and he has a leading amateur in the saddle; high on the list.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; promise at Epsom 13 days ago; up in trip for handicap debut; interesting
runners.owner: Gee Gees Racing, P Gleeson & N Barnes

runners.name: Lenny's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Good start for Adam Kirby when a close third (of 12) in a 1m2f AW handicap at Lingfield in March (for today's rider); not so good back at Lingfield 13 days ago but returning to turf should be in his favour.
runners.comment: Promising stable debut in March (1m2f, AW); return to turf a plus; one to consider

runners.name: Wrist Art
runners.spotlight: Winless since October 2023; placed twice on AW in January but it has been a struggle more recently; 6lb out of the handicap and others are safer.
runners.comment: Struggling in recent starts and not easily recommended from 6lb out of the weights

races.race_name: Fitzdares Telephone & Text Betting Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cape Fear
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three starts, most recently picked off by Fighter's Spirit in the dying strides at Beverley 18 days ago (1m2f, good); 7lb better off today for half a length but he isn't guaranteed to turn the tables on his less-exposed rival.
runners.comment: Second to Fighter's Spirit at Beverley and 7lb better off for 0.5l; in the mix once again

runners.name: My Mate Roger
runners.spotlight: Promising 2yo debut at Kempton in December (1m) and found only the subsequent Dee Stakes runner-up Generic too strong at Yarmouth last month (1m, good); steps up in trip with improvement possible but he is by Blue Point so stamina is not assured.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two 1m runs; solid form claims but he does have to prove his stamina

runners.name: Fighter's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Overcame evident inexperience to make a winning debut in a 1m2f maiden at Beverley (good ground) 18 days ago, a run that can be marked up given he came from a long way back in a steadily-run race; 7lb worse off with the runner-up Cape Fear today but he is open to further progress.
runners.comment: Overcame inexperience to beat Cape Fear by half a length on recent debut; 7lb worse off

runners.name: Barak Warrior
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and ran green when down the field in the Beverley maiden won by Fighter's Spirit 18 days ago; a big step forward is needed.
runners.comment: Finished behind a couple of these in Beverley maiden 18 days ago; big step forward needed
runners.owner: W Sweatman, J Summers, R Foden

runners.name: Probation
runners.spotlight: Ran green when third of six in a Wolverhampton novice (9.4f; Battle Hymn fourth) in March; can do better but he needs to.
runners.comment: Ran green when third of six in a Wolverhampton novice in March; big step forward is needed

runners.name: Relentless Hero
runners.spotlight: Not seen to best effect in a 7f Kempton maiden nine months ago but shaped with clear promise; gelded since; longer trip should be in his favour but he will need to improve to take this; stable also run Cape Fear.
runners.comment: Met trouble when fourth in a 7f AW maiden last August; Cape Fear looks stable's best hope

runners.name: Sax Avoidance
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros foal, 25,000euros yearling; fourth foal of an 8.5f AW winner in France; she will need to be useful to make a winning debut.
runners.comment: 25,000euros yearling; dam an 8.5f AW winner in France; likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Long Reign
runners.spotlight: 125-1 when tailed off in a 1m maiden at Newbury last month; not easily recommended.
runners.comment: 125-1 when tailed off in a 1m maiden at Newbury last month; not easily recommended

runners.name: Billingsgate Gold
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and well beaten in three AW starts this year (1m-1m2f); the addition of cheekpieces isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Poor form in three AW runs and the new cheekpieces aren't enough to tempt

runners.name: Battle Hymn
runners.spotlight: Strong in the betting prior to his Wolverhampton debut in March (9.4f) but he was readily outpointed when the pace increased; clearly thought capable of better and interesting to see if his backers return.
runners.comment: Sent off favourite for his debut in March (9.4f, AW) but could only manage an 8.25l fourth

runners.name: Seraglio Point
runners.spotlight: Looked a longer-term prospect when finishing in midfield in a 1m maiden at Lingfield (AW) 13 days ago; the longer trip is a plus and he is open to improvement.
runners.comment: Open to improvement upped in trip but he looked a longer-term prospect when sixth on debut

runners.name: Mokata
runners.spotlight: Low-level form in two runs this spring (1m and 1m2f); minor handicaps beckon.
runners.comment: Low-level form in two runs this spring (1m and 1m2f); minor handicaps beckon

runners.name: Clough
runners.spotlight: Poor form in two runs last month (1m, good to soft and 7f, good); up in trip with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Poor form in two runs last month; step up in trip not enough to tempt
runners.owner: Matt Broughton, Chris Hughes & D Cunha

runners.name: Mister Pretentious
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two 1m runs last autumn; no appeal.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two 1m runs last autumn; no appeal

races.race_name: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Photosynthesis
runners.spotlight: Slow start to 2026 but there was more encouragement with his fourth of ten at Ascot on Friday (6f, good; well backed); he will need to step up again to take this but it is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Close fourth at Ascot on Friday when well backed; needs to step forward but it's possible

runners.name: Carbine Harvester
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two good AW runs at Kempton; down the field in three AW runs this year but he has had a wind op since last seen; unplaced in four turf runs but the defeats have been in good races and he has shown promise on grass; well drawn and his 7lb lower turf mark heightens interest.
runners.comment: Better on AW (so far) but handicapped accordingly and had wind op since latest run; chance

runners.name: Diligently
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D handicap off this mark last August; two creditable runs this spring and returning to faster ground should suit; chance.
runners.comment: C&D win off this mark last August; two sound runs this year and he's one to consider

runners.name: An Outlaw's Grace
runners.spotlight: Dropping down the weights but with some justification; likely to come good again at some stage but others appeal more today.
runners.comment: Dropping down the weights without looking like capitalising; others appeal more

runners.name: Change Sings
runners.spotlight: Getting on for two years since his last win but he has run two solid races in defeat this year, not ideally drawn when sixth at Newbury last month; effective over C&D and well handicapped; good chance.
runners.comment: On a losing run but conditions to suit and better than latest sixth suggests; good chance

runners.name: Zoulu Chief
runners.spotlight: Shaped okay at Lingfield in January; down the field at Leicester last month but he was harried up front and weakened in the final furlong; dropped 2lb and this easier track will help; one to consider.
runners.comment: Hassled up front when disappointing at Leicester last month; better expected today

runners.name: Badri
runners.spotlight: C&D win off a 3lb higher mark last July; none too consistent on AW over the winter but it wouldn't be a huge surprise to see him bounce back to form returned to turf.
runners.comment: Two good C&D runs last summer; in and out on AW this winter but back to turf on good mark

runners.name: Accrual
runners.spotlight: Had a good spell on AW over the winter but his form has cooled off more recently; drawn widest and others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Two AW wins over the winter but form has cooled more recently; drawn widest back on turf

runners.name: Purest Time
runners.spotlight: Consistency an issue but he has plenty of ability and he is 5lb lower than when last in action on turf, a race where he chased home a promising sprinter at York (6f, good); below par on AW on last two starts but given a three-month break.
runners.comment: On a good mark but consistency an issue; others are safer but too much ability to discount

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: His only win in two years came in Bahrain last March; not beaten far at Nottingham on his recent British return but it took him a long time to pick up; returning to 6f can help; not ruled out for an in-form yard.
runners.comment: Returns to 6f on a good mark and with his yard going well; one to consider

runners.name: Under The Twilight
runners.spotlight: Her last two wins have come over C&D, the latest off this mark last June (good to firm); bumped into one at Leicester 16 days ago and it was a run that promised better; can't rule out.
runners.comment: Her last two wins have come over C&D, the latest off this mark last June; each-way shout

races.race_name: Padel Maidenhead Anniversary Cup Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Startled Lady
runners.spotlight: Showed bright speed from the rail draw over C&D last July, eventually finishing third of 12; not exactly progressive but she looks likely to have a say again following a creditable runner-up finish on Lingfield AW (7f) last month now back at 6f.
runners.comment: Bright speed early when 3rd over C&D last July; creditable return; can have a say

runners.name: Sargent Dennis
runners.spotlight: All three runs at 6f on AW, coming on plenty from a mildly promising debut when runner-up at Wolverhampton and Kempton since; has some unexposed types to contend with today but the bare form gives him good claims.
runners.comment: Solid efforts in defeat on last 2 starts (6f, AW); bare form gives him a good chance

runners.name: Rivera Queen
runners.spotlight: Early signs of ability when third of 12 on debut at Redcar (6f, good to firm) in May 2025 but she wasn't seen again until April, on Southwell AW, when impeded early and never entered the fray; may well need another run and will be eligible for handicaps after today.
runners.comment: Early promise when 3rd at Redcar (6f) last May; never entered the fray on return in April
runners.owner: D Macauliffe, A Don & J Joyce

runners.name: Percy's Star
runners.spotlight: 66-1 for his Leicester debut over 6f last month where he ran fast for a long way and was anything but disgraced behind smart rivals; sights are lowered here and he can be more of a player today.
runners.comment: 66-1 for Leicester debut (6f) but made a promising start; more of a player this time

runners.name: Mooj
runners.spotlight: Found little on Southwell AW in November but her Leicester debut (6f, good; 22-1) the previous month was much more encouraging and she looks one of the more likely ones if the lack of a recent run doesn't hold her back.
runners.comment: Found little on AW in November but debut 3rd over 6f was promising; chance on that run

runners.name: Vicit Venture
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of sprinting blood but he was never in the hunt when soundly beaten on recent Southwell AW debut (7f; 8-1) when hooded and tongue tied; hood discarded now dropped to 6f; has lots to find.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on Southwell AW debut (7f); lots to find dropped to 6f; hood discarded

runners.name: Mayflower Rock
runners.spotlight: 66-1, never recovered from a slow start on Wolverhampton AW debut (6f; Sargent Dennis finished second) last month, finishing well back; likely to need more time.
runners.comment: 66-1, never recovered from a slow start on AW last month; likely to need more time

runners.name: Grand Vista
runners.spotlight: Quite promising Newbury debut (about 6f, soft) in the autumn but he couldn't get to grips with the leaders on Kempton AW (6f; 5-2) when last seen in November; handicaps will be an option after today but he's still of interest back on turf.
runners.comment: Quite promising Newbury debut; not so good on AW after but still of interest back on turf

runners.name: Moravian
runners.spotlight: Unfancied 66-1 shot for recent Lingfield AW debut over 7f and she made no impression; early days yet.
runners.comment: 66-1 shot for recent Lingfield AW debut over 7f and she made no impression; early days

runners.name: Mr Jeffrey
runners.spotlight: 300-1 for Kempton AW debut 11 days ago when finishing more than 30l behind the winner; one to watch for now.
runners.comment: 300-1, beaten over 30l on Kempton AW (7f) 11 days ago

runners.name: Star With Purpose
runners.spotlight: Speedily bred and he showed minor promise from off the pace on his Pontefract debut (6f, good to soft; 6-1) last month; likely to be all the better for that and should improve.
runners.comment: Speedily bred; minor promise on 6f debut at Pontefract (6-1) and should improve

runners.name: Cougar Force
runners.spotlight: Left 2yo form well behind when finishing a close third on handicap debut at Bath (extended 5f, good) last week but that was off a modest mark and she needs so much more back in a novice.
runners.comment: Improved to go close at Bath on handicap debut; much more needed back in a novice

races.race_name: Phil Rendell's Fifty-Tenth Birthday Handicap

runners.name: Cayman Tai
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 28 starts and he has burned some fingers already this year; no doubt he has the ability to win off this mark but the excuses are running out.
runners.comment: Proved expensive to follow this year; capable off this lowly mark but others are safer

runners.name: Correspondence
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden but he has shown some promise and he wasn't beaten far at Bath 12 days ago (5.7f, firm); 1lb lower today and now tried in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-9 but not beaten far at Bath 12 days ago; now tried in cheekpieces

runners.name: Roman Spring
runners.spotlight: Three wins (6f, good to firm (twice) and 5f, AW) last year; didn't run badly on last week's return to action at Salisbury but he will need a step forward to take this.
runners.comment: Three wins last year; encouraging return at Salisbury last week; needs more to take this

runners.name: Diamond Dreamer
runners.spotlight: More prolific on AW than turf; arrives on the back of a solid third at Lingfield two weeks ago (6f, AW); each-way shout.
runners.comment: More prolific on AW than turf; solid third at Lingfield two weeks ago; each-way shout

runners.name: Over Spiced
runners.spotlight: Did well for Grace Harris in 2025 and she has continued the good work this time around, getting off the mark for the year with a game win at Bath last week (5f, good); has a penalty to shoulder but she is well drawn and should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Off the mark for the year with game win at Bath last week; big run on cards from stall 1

runners.name: Merrimack
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last summer; off the mark for 2026 when making all at Chelmsford in January (5f); two defeats since have come over 6f; better expected back at 5f.
runners.comment: C&D winner; made all at Chelmsford in January (5f); subsequent defeats came over 6f

runners.name: Thunder Star
runners.spotlight: Five 5f turf wins on a variety of ground; fair effort on her reappearance (best of the far-side group) but never threatened at Yarmouth last month; she will be well handicapped when her stable find some form.
runners.comment: Will be well handicapped when stable hit some form; others preferred today though

runners.name: Danger Alert
runners.spotlight: Three years since the last of his three wins; kicks off his 2026 campaign off a career-low mark and with his stable going well; cheekpieces the headgear of choice.
runners.comment: Losing run stretches back three years; starts 2026 off career-low mark with yard in form

races.race_name: Rendells Are Hammers Handicap

runners.name: Annastarzy
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well in a good Ascot novice on her return to action ten days ago; more realistic assignment now handicapping and she is one to consider in an open race.
runners.comment: Ran well in an Ascot novice ten days ago; more realistic task now handicapping

runners.name: Akho Mezzna
runners.spotlight: Similar form in four 2yo runs, getting off the mark at Salisbury in October (7f, good); never in contention in a Newmarket handicap on his reappearance (1m, good); cheekpieces now reached for.
runners.comment: 7f win as a 2yo; low-key handicap/seasonal debut last month; now tried in cheekpieces

runners.name: Magical Merlot
runners.spotlight: Ended four-race 2yo campaign with clear win in Brighton novice (1m, good to firm) in October; never looked like winning on his seasonal/handicap debut at Southwell last month; may need more of a test.
runners.comment: 1m Brighton win to round off 2yo campaign; ran okay without threatening on handicap debut

runners.name: Berkshire Boom
runners.spotlight: Down the field on his Salisbury debut last June (6f, good to firm); big step forward when winning at Chester one month later (7f, good) and that form has been handsomely franked; gelded in August; 289-day absence to overcome but almost inconceivable that he's not much better than this mark (the placed horses at Chester are now BHA-rated 78, 90 and 93).
runners.comment: Unexposed and his Chester win last year marks him down as being well ahead of his mark
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Abundant
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three 2yo runs (all at 1m), easily seeing off 13 rivals at Southwell when last seen in October; gelded afterwards; may do better this year but others may prove better treated.
runners.comment: Easy maiden win at Southwell (1m) when last seen in October; gelded since; tough mark

runners.name: Cashbox
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back nurseries in the autumm, over 6f at Lingfield and 7f at Newmarket; gelded prior to his reappearance at Newmarket four weeks ago, a race where he dropped away to finish last of the 13 runners; same mark here and still has to prove his stamina.
runners.comment: Back-to-back nursery wins last autumn; low-key reappearance; stamina not assured

runners.name: Wild Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Ran away with a Redcar handicap on his seasonal return last month (1m, good to firm); defied a penalty at Lingfield (1m; 2-9f) and a double penalty at Kempton (7f; 2-7f) soon after; now 9lb higher against stronger opposition and his mettle will be more fully tested.
runners.comment: Three easy wins since handicapping; up in class with a 9lb rise to overcome

runners.name: Ohara
runners.spotlight: Improved on final 2yo start, comfortably landing a nine-runner nursery at Pontefract (1m, soft) in October; that was his first run on testing ground and it remains to be seen if he can build on it back on a faster surface.
runners.comment: Better for soft ground when last seen, easily beating 8 rivals at Pontefract; this tougher

runners.name: Dartrey
runners.spotlight: Made a mockery of his opening mark, winning three Southwell handicaps in a week at the start of the year (6f/7f); only fourth at Newcastle in his bid for a four-timer but that was a useful contest and his effort was right up there with his best; should stay 1m (half-sister a Group 1 winner at the trip) and his best days could still be ahead of him.
runners.comment: Giant strides in January, winning three times in a week; 1m in range; not fully exposed

runners.name: Midnight Rodeo
runners.spotlight: Improved for a step up in trip and switch to nurseries last summer, rounding off his 2yo campaign with wins at Kempton (1m) and Wolverhampton (9.4f); has more to do in this higher grade off a 7lb higher mark but he isn't fully exposed; returns with his trainer in fine form.
runners.comment: Ended 2yo campaign on the up; 7lb higher in a stronger race but returns with yard in form
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Brave Hunter
runners.spotlight: 20-1 or bigger for his three starts but he has shown a fair level of ability in each of them; steps up to 1m with the prospect of further progress; betting useful now handicapping.
runners.comment: Clear promise in three 7f runs; up in trip for handicap debut; can do better

runners.name: Dumuji
runners.spotlight: Defied market weakness when winning a 7f novice at Kempton on his debut in November; not so good at Leicester four weeks ago; up in trip for his handicap debut but not obviously well treated.
runners.comment: Winning 2yo debut; not so good at Leicester on return; not obviously well treated

runners.name: Here I Bow
runners.spotlight: 7f win in France last year came on soft ground; 20-1 when down the field on her recent stable debut (7f, good to firm); needs to leave that run well behind.
runners.comment: Low-key stable debut (20-1) 11 days ago; still to prove effectiveness on fast turf

races.race_name: Track Radio On Digital & DAB Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Sibling Rivelry
runners.spotlight: Useful strike-rate in a short career, winning three of five starts (AW and turf) since debut and still looked ahead of this mark when successful at Newcastle (about 1m4f) in October; not in the same form on turf after but good to soft ground may not have suited; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Short career, winning 3 of last 5 starts on AW and turf; possible improver as 4yo

runners.name: Lilly Lux
runners.spotlight: Two 1m4f wins on AW in France; winless in Britain and although she's had a couple of close calls on sand at about this trip, she was taken out of a race on good to firm last spring so this surface may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Two 1m4f AW wins in France; running well on sand in Britain; good to firm going is a query

runners.name: Emma's Letter
runners.spotlight: Won 1m Newcastle AW novice as 2yo; has played a part in handicaps since but her return at Leicester, when upped to 1m4f (cheekpieces replaced blinkers, return today), leaves her well short of what will be needed here.
runners.comment: Won over 1m on AW as 2yo but she was soundly beaten upped to 1m4f on return

runners.name: Sea Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who has finished fourth and first in two C&D starts, latterly when making all last August (good to firm); has added two more wins since, on turf and AW, most recently in February, and while not obviously ahead of her mark she shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Multiple wins include one over C&D (good to firm) last August; can give a good account

runners.name: Mrembo
runners.spotlight: All five wins on good or quicker ground, including firm; C&D winner who finished close up behind one who was ahead of her mark in this race last year and she's likely to be primed for this; leading claims for in-form yard.
runners.comment: C&D winner; close 3rd to well-treated rival in this last year; solid for in-form yard

runners.name: Society Girl
runners.spotlight: Kept on nicely into fourth of ten on Yarmouth debut (1m, good to soft) last October but failed to repeat it on the switch to AW on final 2yo start; highly tried on her Newbury return and this handicap debut looks more her level with her pedigree suggesting the new trip will suit.
runners.comment: Good 2yo debut; highly tried on return; can improve over new trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Pershalla
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since 2yo debut but she improved for the step up to 1m4f and the fitting of a hood when winning on Kempton AW last month and she had more to do off 4lb higher when second to an odds-on shot at Yarmouth (about 1m3f, good) 13 days ago; probably needs more for the win.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; improved for step up to 1m4f/hood when winning on AW; creditable 2nd since

runners.name: Kindly Queen
runners.spotlight: Well-connected filly who looked every inch a stayer over a similar trip to this when a never-nearer fourth on handicap debut at Kempton (AW) in March; a greater test than this could suit in due course but the likely good pace will help and perhaps fitting blinkers will also be a plus.
runners.comment: Looked a stayer when a keeping-on 4th on handicap debut (AW; 1m4f); can improve

runners.name: Pangbourne
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in her first two AW handicaps at about 1m and 1m1f; lesser effort since but there's plenty of stamina in her pedigree and it's possible this new trip might be a big positive on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up in first two AW handicaps; not so good latest; bred to stay well; turf debut

runners.name: Queen Of Astolat
runners.spotlight: Beaten 14l+ in three starts at about 1m and 1m2f late last year (hooded each time but not today); bred to appreciate the extra stamina test now handicapping but hard to know how much she'll need the run.
runners.comment: Beaten 14l+ in all three starts; bred to improve for this trip on handicap debut

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cydney Sweenie
runners.spotlight: 9,000gns foal, 25,000gns yearling who fared best of the newcomers on her turf debut three weeks ago (third successful since); extra furlong will suit on that evidence and she holds obvious claims.
runners.comment: Best of the newcomers on debut three weeks ago; extra furlong will suit; obvious claims

runners.name: Dun Eideann
runners.spotlight: 13,000gns yearling whose dam didn't make a mark until handicapping as a 3yo; trainer had a quiet spring thus far but had a well-backed 2yo newcomer win on turf nine days ago.
runners.comment: Trainer had a quiet spring but had a well-backed 2yo newcomer win on turf nine days ago

runners.name: Agnes Hathaway
runners.spotlight: Solid twice as a yearling, fetching 16,500gns, then 25,000gns; out of an unraced half-sister to several winners; taken out of her intended debut late last month due to running a temperature.
runners.comment: Solid twice as a yearling, latterly for 25,000gns; out of a half-sister to several winners
runners.owner: D Kilburn, C Wright, F Mahony & Partner

runners.name: Kach Above
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal of Kachess, who won on her 2yo debut before regressing; not best drawn on debut and the market will show what's expected.
runners.comment: Not best drawn on debut and the market will show what's expected

runners.name: Bad Baker
runners.spotlight: 33,000gns yearling whose dam won as a 2yo for Rod Millman; might have been better drawn on debut and the market will be the best guide.
runners.comment: 33,000gns yearling whose dam won as a 2yo for Rod Millman; might have been better drawn

runners.name: Blake's Monarch
runners.spotlight: April-foaled £20,000 yearling who's a half-brother to a fair nursery winner (5f) in Sabrimento; holds a couple of sales race entries later on, for a yard that boasts an ordinary strike-rate with juveniles (this their first 2yo runner of the season).
runners.comment: April-foaled £20,000 yearling; holds a couple of sales race entries later on
runners.owner: The Good Racing Co Ltd & Partners

runners.name: Emerald Bay
runners.spotlight: Gelded 42,000euros yearling who's the first foal of a French middie-distance winner; yard going great guns of late, including with 2yos, but whether an easy 6f from the outside stall proves optimal remains to be seen.
runners.comment: Gelded 42,000euros yearling; easy 6f from the outside stall may not prove optimal on debut

runners.name: Indian Land
runners.spotlight: Powerful yard had a number beaten at short prices of late and this well-related colt ran well below market expectations on his turf debut recently (split two of these at Leicester); evidently felt capable of better.
runners.comment: Below market expectations on debut (split two of these); evidently felt capable of better

runners.name: Parading
runners.spotlight: Pedigree a mix of speed and stamina; easy to back, missed the break and showed signs of greenness on her turf debut a couple of weeks ago; tough draw but trainer boasts a fine record with 2yos on the AW.
runners.comment: Easy to back, missed the break and showed signs of greenness on her turf debut

runners.name: Taseem
runners.spotlight: 80-1 outsider of six, ran as the market suggested he would when finishing a remote last at Yarmouth three weeks ago; withdrawn last weekend having got loose at the start at Doncaster (50-1 at the time).
runners.comment: 80-1 outsider of six, ran as the market suggested he would when well beaten on debut

runners.name: Allaboutthelunch
runners.spotlight: 4,000gns yearling who has stamina on the dam's side of his pedigree; 40-1, finished last of 12 when behind Parading and Indian Land on his Leicester debut; may want more time.
runners.comment: 40-1, finished last of 12 when behind Parading and Indian Land on his Leicester debut

runners.name: Crystal Kraken
runners.spotlight: Started a three-figure price and finished well beaten after a slow start on her recent Bath debut; needs to improve a lot on that.
runners.comment: Three-figure price and finished well beaten after a slow start on her recent Bath debut

races.race_name: Advance Price Monday Racing For £13 Handicap

runners.name: Fine Print
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner last spring who was well fancied over C&D on Friday, only to overrace in a messy affair; needs considering under Oisin Murphy, who's taking a first ride for the trainer (always to be respected with runners here).
runners.comment: Dual course winner last spring; things didn't pan out here on Friday; on the shortlist

runners.name: Starshine Legend
runners.spotlight: Among the favourites on his debut for this yard, back from over 18 months off, at Pontefract early last month; cracked late there, having made a lot of the running, and is entitled to improve; claims going back up in trip having been dropped 2lb.
runners.comment: Entitled to improve for his comeback; claims going back up in trip having been dropped 2lb

runners.name: Lednikov
runners.spotlight: Six-time Southwell winner, latterly by a wide margin in an apprentice handicap in March (handicapper punished him for that); finished held twice since and his record round here is nothing like as good.
runners.comment: Six-time Southwell winner; record round here is nothing like as good

runners.name: Green Team
runners.spotlight: On the downgrade in recent times for Ian Williams, from whom he was picked up for 7,500gns last month, his latest second coming in a messy, small-field affair; decent record here, though, and is one to consider under regular partner Billy Loughnane.
runners.comment: Decent record here and is one to consider under Billy Loughnane for his new yard

runners.name: Spec Of Light
runners.spotlight: Regressive gelding who's lacked consistency of late, including in two forms of headgear; having a first crack at beyond 1m4f and needs to improve for the stiffer test.
runners.comment: Having a first crack at beyond 1m4f and needs to improve for the stiffer test

runners.name: Tuba
runners.spotlight: Likes it here, landing a second course win, again from the front for Gina Mangan, over C&D six weeks ago; was allowed his own way that night and this is more competitive if anything but he holds claims upped a fair 4lb.
runners.comment: Likes it here; this is more competitive if anything but he holds claims upped a fair 4lb

runners.name: Sneaky Blinder
runners.spotlight: Three wins here over the extended 2m in recent times, latterly off 2lb lower; the return to Tapeta will suit, off the back of two ordinary efforts, but others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Three wins here over extended 2m in recent times, latterly off 2lb lower; others stronger

runners.name: V Power
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent but made it 2-3 over C&D, and 2-3 for Zoe Lewis, off a 3lb lower mark in a first-time tongue-tie five weeks ago (previously behind Tuba); player if in the same mood.
runners.comment: Made it 2-3 over C&D, and 2-3 for Zoe Lewis, off 3lb lower latest; not the most consistent

runners.name: Arch Legend
runners.spotlight: Course winner for Charlie Johnston; struggled for this yard last summer and returns from nine months off tonight; did win first time out as a 3yo but would want to see some support before considering him.
runners.comment: Struggled for this yard last summer and returns from nine months off tonight
runners.owner: S Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Daaris
runners.spotlight: Three wins, two seconds and two thirds to his name from nine runs over C&D; will need a career-best off this mark, though, and there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh (off since January).
runners.comment: Three wins, two seconds and two thirds from nine runs over C&D; high in the weights now

races.race_name: Supporting Mental Health Awareness Week Handicap

runners.name: Crafter
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who's been brightened up by the blinkers of late without having the breaks; a slow start cost him here a month ago and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; a slow start cost him here a month ago and he's one to consider

runners.name: Wicklow Way
runners.spotlight: Supported when winning a Newcastle 0-50 on his comeback in March; since finished a remote last on turf but pulled hard in better company and he ought to fare better back on Tapeta.
runners.comment: Remote last on turf latest but pulled hard; ought to fare better back on Tapeta

runners.name: Wave Rock
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 3lb lower in February who pulled away her chances next time before coming back in form latest; one to look at closely under Oisin Murphy, who's 11-36 for the yard.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 3lb lower in February; considered under Oisin Murphy (11-36 for the yard)

runners.name: Relevant Range
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a successful winter on Tapeta at a lowly level and is back on a workable mark after a couple of runs on turf; inclined to pull hard and hasn't seen it out in a handful of tries over this sort of trip though.
runners.comment: Inclined to pull hard and hasn't seen it out in a handful of tries over this sort of trip

runners.name: Arlecchino's Rex
runners.spotlight: Running consistently round here all year at the bottom level; no obvious reason he won't get involved again but this is a touch stronger having been nudged up another 2lb.
runners.comment: Running consistently round here all year at the bottom level; should go well again

runners.name: Brinton
runners.spotlight: Got into the habit of blowing the start but in fair form, adding to her trainer's good recent run when winning on turf last time; has not looked to see it out in a couple of previous tries over this far, though, and is drawn widest upped 3lb.
runners.comment: Got into the habit of blowing the start but been in fair form; longer trip a concern

runners.name: Book Of Life
runners.spotlight: Hard to win with but usually gives her running round here and arrives in form; will want to settle better back up in trip but holds each-way claims otherwise.
runners.comment: Hard to win with but usually gives her running round here and is in form; each-way shout

runners.name: Tiger Beetle
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who's getting on and been on the downgrade since missing a year; found his level again lately but is some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner who's getting on and been on the downgrade since missing a year

runners.name: Beat The Odds
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure prices for three runs spanning around a year, finishing well beaten each time; this more realistic but has little to recommend her.
runners.comment: Triple-figure prices for three runs spanning about a year, finishing well beaten each time

runners.name: Molly Valentine
runners.spotlight: Lost her way for Dean Ivory last backend and has struggled in two runs for two yards this spring; would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Lost her way for Dean Ivory and has struggled in two runs for two yards this spring

runners.name: Merapi
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who's found her level for this yard, twice finishing second on turf last month, including over this sort of trip for the first time latest; needs to improve on previous AW work if she's to make it 14th time lucky.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden who's found her level for this yard; has to improve on previous AW work

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Celestra
runners.spotlight: Last July's two seconds well advertised since and she started odds-on for her Beverley comeback last month; came right away with her market rival that day and sets the standard on her AW debut.
runners.comment: Came right away with her market rival on comeback and sets the standard on her AW debut

runners.name: Wild Violet
runners.spotlight: Refused to enter the stalls on her intended Newbury debut last month (40-1 at the time; Musical Accord started 17-2); just a fair first run at Newmarket ten days ago, when well held in third, and she'll need a decent step forward.
runners.comment: Just a fair debut at Newmarket, when well held in third; needs a decent step forward

runners.name: She Commands
runners.spotlight: 450,000gns purchase who needed her debut experience on turf last August; bettered that when coming away with a stablemate of Celestra's on Polytrack next time and should have more to offer under a penalty.
runners.comment: 450,000gns purchase who should have more to offer under a penalty; respected

runners.name: Hot Silk
runners.spotlight: Big price for one start last autumn, when shaping nicely despite hanging left; didn't build on that in a small-field Southwell maiden on her comeback early last month, when again inclined to lug left; tonight's longer trip should suit on pedigree.
runners.comment: Again inclined to lug left on comeback; tonight's longer trip should suit on pedigree

runners.name: Six Blue
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard when tailed off in three bumpers; fared better in two runs on the AW but tonight's drop in trip doesn't appear optimal and her future probably lies in low-grade handicaps.
runners.comment: Drop in trip doesn't appear optimal and her future probably lies in low-grade handicaps

runners.name: Musical Accord
runners.spotlight: 350,000gns yearling who missed the break from a wide stall when well below market expectations on her Newbury debut last month; should do better now she's gained some experience for a yard also responsible for She Commands.
runners.comment: Below market expectations on her Newbury debut last month; should do better

races.race_name: Racing Welfare Supporting Racing's Workforce Handicap

runners.name: Street Dancer
runners.spotlight: Offered more on her handicap debut here (7.2f) and took another step forward over C&D last Tuesday; the lightest raced of these and has Oisin Murphy up, so has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Another step forward over C&D last Tuesday; the lightest raced of these; player

runners.name: Captain Pickles
runners.spotlight: Touch unlucky not to notch his fourth win of the year when beaten a short-head at Southwell a month ago; 1lb rise puts him on the ceiling mark for this class of race and he has a fair chance of making it 3-4 over C&D.
runners.comment: Touch unlucky last time and has a fair chance of making it 3-4 over C&D

runners.name: South Kensington
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner last autumn who hasn't achieved much in a few runs over 1m; ran better than her final position on turf last time but stamina would be a worry.
runners.comment: Dual 7f winner last autumn who hasn't achieved much in a few runs over 1m; stamina worries

runners.name: Aspire To Glory
runners.spotlight: Won four handicaps for Alice Haynes last year (one over C&D), latterly off this mark, despite often lugging left; not offered a lot in two runs back for his current yard and needs a revival.
runners.comment: Won four handicaps for Alice Haynes last year; not offered a lot in two runs back

runners.name: She's A Goldigger
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden who's still to convince over this far; didn't offer much on her Redcar comeback three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden who's still to convince over this far; didn't offer much on her comeback

runners.name: Simba's Gift
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden who reared as the stalls opened on turf last time and did well to get as close; that came off the back of a solid stable debut over C&D and he's shortlisted under Billy Loughnane (1-9 for the yard).
runners.comment: Reared as the stalls opened latest; shortlisted under Billy Loughnane (1-9 for the yard)

runners.name: Port Louis
runners.spotlight: Regressive in Ireland and been patchy for this yard; much lower in the weights than on turf but for a reason and he's struggled in two runs since his big-priced Doncaster second.
runners.comment: Patchy for this yard; much lower in the weights than on turf but for a reason

runners.name: So Chic
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent mare who's usually ridden patiently over shorter; came home well over 7.2f here last time but is no certainty to build on that on just her second try over this far.
runners.comment: Inconsistent mare who's usually ridden patiently over shorter; capable but risky

runners.name: Princess Vivi
runners.spotlight: Poor maiden who offered more for the cheekpieces going on in March, when twice chasing home Captain Pickles, latterly from the front over C&D; 4lb better off tonight but others are more convincing.
runners.comment: More for cheekpieces going on in March, when twice behind Captain Pickles; each-way shout

runners.name: Scarfo
runners.spotlight: Long time since his sole win and hasn't offered much this year; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Long time since his sole win and hasn't offered much this year; hard to make a case for

runners.name: Red Admiral
runners.spotlight: Couple of expensive defeats for George Boughey early last year before being sold for 4,600gns, looking anything but easy; pulled away his chances on comeback, back up to 1m, despite a first-time hood and only has daylight on his outer from stall 13.
runners.comment: Anything but easy; pulled away his chances on comeback; may again from stall 13

runners.name: Love You More
runners.spotlight: Often compromised her chances last year by starting slowly and/or pulling hard; solid turf comeback, when a clear second off a 2lb lower mark, gave something to build on.
runners.comment: Solid turf comeback, when a clear second off a 2lb lower mark, gave something to build on

runners.name: Wyvern
runners.spotlight: Does most racing now at Newcastle, the scene of his last three wins; off for six weeks since finishing well held behind Captain Pickles over C&D.
runners.comment: Off for six weeks since finishing well held behind Captain Pickles over C&D

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Lady Milton
runners.spotlight: Trainer has enjoyed a fine fortnight and this filly, who made it 3-3 in handicaps on her comeback four weeks ago, has every chance of adding another win to the tally; upped 6lb for landing that messy Lingfield affair but won't mind coming back down in trip.
runners.comment: Made it 3-3 in handicaps on her comeback; won't mind coming back down in trip; shortlisted

runners.name: Eastern Veil
runners.spotlight: Still needed the experience when narrowly winning her maiden over C&D last autumn; solid comeback under a penalty in a Polytrack novice five weeks ago, when hitting traffic, and her claims are obvious.
runners.comment: Solid comeback under a penalty in a Polytrack novice (hit traffic); claims are obvious

runners.name: Searchingtheblues
runners.spotlight: Won her first two as a 2yo, including easily round here, and entered handicaps on an unrealistic mark; best efforts of late have come on Tapeta, including down to this company for the first time latest, and she's considered despite having stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Best efforts of late have come on Tapeta; considered despite having stamina to prove

runners.name: Silky Lass
runners.spotlight: Doubtful she needed to improve on previous placed efforts to win an ordinary Newcastle novice in February; probably best judged on her Doncaster handicap debut (1m2f) recently, when well beaten; others are open to greater improvement.
runners.comment: Well beaten on her Doncaster handicap debut (1m2f); others are open to greater improvement

runners.name: Kilkenny Warrior
runners.spotlight: Well treated when entering nursery company last autumn, twice winning easily (6f-7f); tougher going off her revised mark this spring but her latest effort, and the dam's side of her pedigree, gives hope this longer trip will be within range.
runners.comment: Tougher going off her revised mark this spring; needs more for the longer trip

runners.name: Squiffy
runners.spotlight: Found a weak, small-field maiden when winning here (7.2f) under Billy Loughnane (8-35 for the yard) five months ago; looks on a sensible mark now handicapping and remains open to improvement; market informative.
runners.comment: On a sensible mark now handicapping and remains open to improvement; market informative

runners.name: Crystal Pier
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress last year, latterly finishing a well-beaten last of three upped to 1m2f; this half-sister to a Polytrack and a Dirt winner needs to improve on her AW debut back from seven months off.
runners.comment: Didn't progress last year; needs to improve on her AW debut back from seven months off
